text: Emit and hydrate supervisor state-machine state across all session runtimes
description: Ensure every supported session runtime exposes the current state-engine state (for example EXPLORING, WORKING, WRAPPING_UP, ERROR) on connect and on transition, and ensure the frontend can hydrate that state reliably instead of falling back to generic connection labels.

reflections.context: The UI needed a more reliable way to display the current supervisor state than just generic connection labels.
reflections.outcome: State machine states are now consistently reported and displayed in the watchdog panel.
reflections.effectiveness: Standardizing the supervisor-state emission event across all agent session wrappers allowed for a unified hydration pattern in the frontend. This significantly improved UI feedback during state transitions.
reflections.lesson: Explicit state machine state reporting is essential for complex autonomous systems to remain transparent and debuggable for human operators.

status.comment: Implemented supervisor-state emissions across all session wrappers (Claude, Gemini, Codex) and added hydration logic to the frontend store. UI now reflects real state-engine state on connect and transition. (RE-APPLYING DONE STATUS)
